Now let me also check if there's an issue with the CPU usage calculation logic. Looking at the code, I suspect the issue might be in how the percentages are being calculated. Let me examine the FreeRTOS runtime stats format more carefully and fix the calculation:


---

Tool use: **web_search**

<details>
            <summary>Searched web `FreeRTOS vTaskGetRunTimeStats output format ESP32 ESP-IDF` • **1** results</summary>
        
| Title | URL | Chunk |
|-------|-----|-------|
| Web Search Results |  | The issue of CPU statistics displaying as 0.0% on your ESP32 diagnostics page can stem from several factors:<br/><br/>1. **Task Execution and Idle Time**: In FreeRTOS, which the ESP32 utilizes, the system's idle tasks often consume a significant portion of CPU time when the device isn't performing other tasks. This can result in user-defined tasks showing minimal or zero CPU usage. For instance, in a FreeRTOS test report, the idle tasks accounted for a substantial percentage of CPU time, while other tasks showed minimal usage. ([matrix.ruyisdk.org](https://matrix.ruyisdk.org/reports/ESP32P4-FreeRTOS-README/?utm_source=openai))<br/><br/>2. **Measurement Methodology**: The method used to calculate CPU usage can influence the displayed statistics. If the diagnostics page relies on specific FreeRTOS functions like `uxTaskGetSystemState()`, the reported CPU usage might vary based on the sampling period and system activity. Variations in runtime statistics have been observed in similar contexts. ([forum.esp32.com](https://forum.esp32.com/viewtopic.php?t=1532&utm_source=openai))<br/><br/>3. **System Configuration and Updates**: Changes in system configuration or firmware updates can affect how CPU metrics are collected and displayed. For example, certain releases have been associated with high system load issues on ESP32 devices, which could impact diagnostics reporting. ([github.com](https://github.com/letscontrolit/ESPEasy/issues/4485?utm_source=openai))<br/><br/>**Recommendations**:<br/><br/>- **Review Task Priorities and Execution**: Ensure that your tasks are correctly prioritized and actively executing as intended. If tasks are dormant or waiting indefinitely, they may not register significant CPU usage.<br/><br/>- **Verify Measurement Tools**: Confirm that the tools or functions used to measure CPU usage are appropriate and correctly implemented. Consider using FreeRTOS's built-in functions like `vTaskGetRunTimeStats()` to obtain more accurate runtime statistics.<br/><br/>- **Check System Updates**: If you've recently updated your ESP32 firmware or made configuration changes, review the release notes or documentation to identify any alterations that might affect CPU usage reporting.<br/><br/>By systematically examining these areas, you can identify and address the factors contributing to the 0.0% CPU usage readings on your diagnostics page. |

</details>

+                     percent_start = strstr(percent_start + 1, "\t");
+                     if (percent_start) {
+                         float percent = atof(percent_start + 1);
+                         
+                         // Add to non-idle if not an idle task
+                         if (strstr(line, "IDLE") == NULL) {
+                             non_idle_percentage += percent;
+                         }
+                     }
+                 }
+                 
+                 line = strchr(line, '\n');
+                 if (line) {
+                     line++;
+                 } else {
+                     break;
+                 }
+             }
+             
+             // Use the non-idle percentage directly
+             s_cpu_usage_percent = non_idle_percentage;
